nodes.label: M004: Bill & Checkout
Phase: validating-milestone
M001: Project Foundation
S01: Next.js + Tailwind + Prisma Setup
T01: Scaffold Next.js 16 project and install all dependencies
T02: Initialize Prisma 7 with SQLite adapter and create client singleton
T03: Build Vietnamese TraSua landing page and verify full stack
S02: Database Schema & Seed Data
T01: Replace placeholder schema with full ordering models and run migration
T02: Write idempotent seed script with Vietnamese menu data and 15 tables
S03: QR Code Generator
T01: Install QR/PDF deps, configure env vars, build admin auth with login page, and bundle Vietnamese font
T02: Build QR PDF generation API route with pdfkit and qrcode at /api/admin/qr-pdf
T03: Build Vietnamese admin page at /admin with QR generator UI and PDF download button
M002: Customer Order Flow
S01: Menu Browsing — QR to Tabbed Menu Display
T01: VND Formatter Utility + Order Page with Table Validation
T02: Tabbed Menu View with Item Cards
S02: Cart + Order Submission — Full Ordering Flow
T01: Cart State Provider with sessionStorage Persistence
T02: Sticky Cart Bar + Slide-Up Cart Sheet
T03: Order Creation API + Submission + Confirmation
M003: Staff Dashboard
S01: Bar Station End-to-End (SSE + API + UI)
T01: SSE subscriber registry + order status derivation
T02: SSE stream endpoint + GET orders endpoint
T03: PATCH item status endpoint + SSE broadcast on order creation
T04: useOrderStream hook + bar station page UI
S02: Kitchen + Overview Stations + Item Cancellation
T01: Create kitchen page, overview page, and staff layout with navigation tabs
T02: Fix PATCH endpoint to recalculate totalAmount on item cancellation and add test
T03: Add cancel button with two-tap confirmation to OrderCard ItemRow
S03: Notifications & Auto-Hide Polish
T01: Add notification chime with visual pulse and mute toggle
T02: Add auto-hide completed items with Lịch sử toggle and disconnection banner
S01: Bill & Payment Flow
T01: Schema migration + order-status guards + staff orders PAID exclusion
T02: Bill aggregation API + mark-paid API
T03: Checkout page UI — table list + bill detail + payment
T04: SSE payment integration + useOrderStream extension + unit tests
S02: Add Items from Bill
T01: Create GET /api/staff/menu and POST /api/staff/orders/[orderId]/items endpoints with unit tests
T02: Build MenuPickerModal and wire into BillView with full flow verification
M005: Admin & Polish

**Full ordering schema upfront (4 models + 3 enums) rather than incremental** — Defined MenuItem, Table, Order, OrderItem with Category, OrderStatus, ItemStatus enums all in M001, even though Order/OrderItem aren't used until M002. One clean migration covers everything; downstream milestones just consume.

**PDFKit + qrcode for QR PDF generation (D004)** — Pure Node.js pipeline: qrcode.toBuffer() for PNG → PDFKit for A4 layout. Rejected jspdf (browser-first) and @react-pdf/renderer (heavyweight). Both packages are offline-compatible per R006.
**Edge-compatible cookie auth for admin (D005)** — ADMIN_PASSWORD env var + httpOnly cookie. Middleware on Edge runtime protects /admin/* routes. Proportionate to local-network threat model.
**@@map() on all Prisma models** — Essential when model names collide with SQL reserved words (Order → orders, Table → tables). Applied to all 4 models proactively.
**Int prices for VND** — Vietnamese đồng has no decimal usage, so Int type is simpler and more correct than Float/Decimal for all price fields.
**create-next-app rejects capital-letter directory names** — TraSua fails validation. Workaround: scaffold into temp lowercase subdirectory, copy files up, fix package.json name field.
**Prisma 7 introduces prisma.config.ts** — Seed command configuration goes in the migrations block of prisma.config.ts, not in package.json scripts. The file is auto-generated by `prisma init` in v7.

**Scripts outside Next.js can't use @/ path alias** — seed.ts and other standalone scripts must use relative imports (e.g. '../generated/prisma/client') because they're executed via tsx, not within Next.js's module resolution.
**Next.js Route Handlers default to Edge runtime** — Must set explicit `runtime = 'nodejs'` when using Node.js APIs like pdfkit's Buffer/fs. Otherwise the route silently fails or throws at runtime.

**PrismaClient singleton via globalThis** — src/lib/prisma.ts caches the client on globalThis in non-production to survive HMR reloads. All code imports from `@/lib/prisma` — never instantiates PrismaClient directly.
**PDFKit buffer-collection for Next.js** — Collect chunks in array, await 'end' promise, then Buffer.concat() and pass to new Response(). pipe() is incompatible with Next.js Response constructor.

**Prisma seed idempotency via deleteMany → recreate** — deleteMany in reverse FK order (orderItem → order → menuItem → table) then Promise.all create. Simpler than upsert when seed data doesn't need ID stability.

**Cookie-based admin auth with Edge middleware** — src/middleware.ts protects /admin/* and /api/admin/* via config.matcher, exempting login routes. Cookie helpers in src/lib/auth.ts are Edge-compatible (no Node.js APIs).

**Prisma 7 config restructuring** — prisma.config.ts was unexpected from `prisma init`. Seed command, datasource URL, and other config moved from package.json/schema.prisma into this new file. Required plan adaptation.

**GitHub raw URLs redirect to HTML for large repos** — Attempted to download Inter TTF from GitHub raw content URL, but large repos redirect to an HTML page instead. Google Fonts static CDN (gstatic.com) is the reliable alternative.

**Deleting old migrations cleaner than incremental** — When S02 replaced the placeholder Category-only schema with 4 models, attempting incremental migration from the placeholder was problematic. Deleting the old migrations directory entirely and running a fresh `migrate dev` was cleaner and more reliable.

**Two-slice decomposition along read/write boundary:** Split M002 into S01 (menu browsing — read path) and S02 (cart + order submission — write path) instead of three slices with a standalone API slice. The read path is independently valuable (QR codes lead to a real menu), and the API belongs with the cart because they form a single demoable capability.

**Route Handler over Server Action for order creation:** Used POST /api/order with JSON request/response and explicit status codes (400/404/409) instead of Server Actions. Cart submission is a JSON payload from client-side state, not a form — Server Actions would require workarounds for custom error shapes.

**Server-side totalAmount computation — never trust client:** POST /api/order always re-computes totalAmount from DB prices with full FK validation chain. This prevents price manipulation and ensures data integrity regardless of client state.

**CartUI children-wrapping with ViewState discriminated union:** Restructured CartUI to accept children (server components) and manage menu↔confirmation transitions via ViewState, keeping page.tsx as a server component. This deviation from the original T02 plan was necessary because the confirmation screen swap required a client boundary that could coordinate both views.

**Children-wrapping pattern for client boundaries:** When a server component page needs client-side view transitions, wrap server component children in a client wrapper managing ViewState — avoids converting the page itself to a client component. This was discovered during S02/T03 when the confirmation screen swap demanded a unified client boundary.

**sessionStorage keyed by domain entity prevents cross-entity contamination:** Keying cart persistence by `trasua-cart-{tableId}` ensures table isolation with zero additional infrastructure. Simple but effective — the key naming convention is the whole pattern.

**Unavailable item handling needs seed data for visual QA:** The 'Hết hàng' badge was code-verified but never visually confirmed in browser because no seed data items have `available: false`. For future milestones, seed data should cover all UI states, not just the happy path.

**CartProvider useReducer + sessionStorage hydration guard:** Five-action reducer (ADD_ITEM, REMOVE_ITEM, UPDATE_QUANTITY, UPDATE_NOTES, CLEAR_CART) with payload wrapper objects. sessionStorage keyed by tableId. `useIsHydrated` guard prevents initial empty state from overwriting stored cart on mount. Reusable for any client-side state needing persistence across page navigations.

**Spring-like cubic-bezier(0.32, 0.72, 0, 1) for staggered entrance animations:** Used for OrderConfirmation's 5-element staggered fadeSlideUp (0→350ms delays) and CartSheet slide-up. Produces a natural, spring-like deceleration that feels polished on mobile. Reuse for any entrance animation in the app.

**Server-side price computation with full FK validation chain:** API route validates: body shape → quantity positivity → table existence → menuItem existence → menuItem availability, then computes totals from DB prices. Each validation returns a specific HTTP status code. This chain pattern should be applied to all order-modifying endpoints.

**Dead-end error page pattern for invalid URL params:** ErrorPage renders a full-screen Vietnamese message with no navigation links — no escape routes to valid content. Prevents users from reaching ordering functionality without a valid QR scan. Apply this pattern to any entry-point validation.

**CartUI required restructuring mid-slice:** The original T02 plan had CartUI as a thin sibling wrapper, but the confirmation screen (T03) needed a client boundary that could manage both MenuView and OrderConfirmation rendering — this forced a restructure to a children-wrapping pattern with ViewState. The deviation was small in code but conceptually important: it revealed that view-transition requirements should be considered during slice planning, not discovered during task implementation.

The deviation was small in code but conceptually important: it revealed that view-transition requirements should be considered during slice planning, not discovered during task implementation.

**Station-first vertical slice decomposition (D008):** Three slices ordered by risk — S01 proves SSE with the bar station end-to-end, S02 extends the proven StationView pattern to kitchen/overview + cancel, S03 adds purely additive polish. This de-risked SSE technology before investing in station expansion.

**SSE subscriber registry on globalThis with station-level filtering (D009):** Single SSE endpoint at /api/staff/orders/stream with ?station= query param. Registry caches on globalThis for HMR safety. Broadcasts filter by station to reduce client noise.

**Shared StationView component parameterized by station type (D010):** All three stations share the same data shape, real-time mechanism, and card layout. Only filtering and available transitions differ. A single parameterized component avoids triplicate code and ensures change propagation.

**Cancel items only — R008 partial delivery:** M003 delivers item cancellation from dashboard. Adding items to existing orders deferred to M005. Customers use "Gọi thêm món" (new order for same table) as interim.

**Enriched SSE payloads eliminate client-side API roundtrips:** Including full menuItem details + table info in SSE events means station UIs can render and filter without additional GET calls. Design SSE events to be self-sufficient — enriching at broadcast time is cheaper than N client-side API roundtrips.

**StaffNav must be extracted as Client Component for usePathname while keeping layout as Server Component:** Next.js App Router requires careful server/client boundary management. The layout.tsx stays as Server Component for better performance; the navigation component that needs `usePathname()` is extracted into its own client component file.

**Windows compatibility for verification commands:** Unix-specific shell commands like `test -f` in verification scripts fail on Windows development environments. Cross-platform alternatives (or Node.js fs.existsSync checks) should be used in task plan verification steps.

**SSE subscriber registry on globalThis with dead subscriber cleanup:** Registry pattern at src/lib/sse.ts — addSubscriber/removeSubscriber/broadcast/getSubscriberCount. Dead subscribers auto-cleaned during broadcast iteration. Station-level metadata on each subscriber for filtered broadcast.

**Order status auto-derivation from item statuses (computed, not stored):** deriveOrderStatus() computes on read and write as single source of truth. All PENDING→PENDING, any PREPARING→PREPARING, all non-cancelled READY→READY, all SERVED→SERVED, all CANCELLED→CANCELLED.

**Three-bucket order display with time-based auto-transitions:** categorizeOrders pure function splits orders into active/recentlyCompleted/hidden buckets. Injectable time parameters enable deterministic testing. 30-second bucketTick interval balances precision and performance.

**Ref-based callback stabilization for SSE hooks:** onNewOrderRef stores callback in a ref so useEffect dependency array doesn't include callback identity. Prevents SSE reconnection churn when parent re-renders with new callback reference.

**Two-tap confirmation UX for destructive actions on touch interfaces:** First tap shows confirmation prompt ('Xác nhận huỷ?'), second tap executes. 3-second auto-reset timer via useEffect+useRef. Loading spinner prevents double-submission.

**SSE in Next.js 16 App Router worked smoothly with zero friction:** ReadableStream + text/event-stream headers worked out of the box. The globalThis subscriber registry pattern (matching Prisma singleton) survived HMR reloads. No connection lifecycle issues in dev mode despite initial risk assessment flagging this as high-risk.

**Stale .next/dev/types/ auto-generated files cause TypeScript errors when pages are added without the dev server running:** The files go stale and report type errors for new pages. Deleting .next/dev/types/ before running tsc --noEmit resolves it. This is a Windows development environment gotcha.
